candidate_problems.text: Specify criteria under which one equivalence relation over ships should be privileged in practical or metaphysical contexts rather than treating all as equally admissible.
Analyze mixed identity criteria that weight matter, form, function, and causal history, and determine whether they yield stable verdicts or context-sensitive indeterminacy.
Extend the framework to cases with branching continuities—such as reconstructed-from-original-parts versus continuously repaired objects—and test whether any non-arbitrary identity rule handles both.
Determine whether there are domains where the bare description itself fixes the relevant invariant, contrary to the claim of underdetermination.
